title: CIP-309 encryption recipient slot
description: A single per-recipient slot in a sealed-PoE envelope, carrying the content-encryption key wrapped to one recipient. The slot shape is KEM-DRIVEN, but the envelope-level `kem` identifier is not visible from a slot in isolation, so this schema is deliberately PERMISSIVE: `epk`, `kem_ct`, and `wrap` are all OPTIONAL, the map is NOT closed, and no cross-KEM exclusion is asserted. The two registered shapes are: classical x25519 = `{ epk: bstr(32), wrap: bstr(48) }` (ephemeral X25519 public key + wrapped CEK); hybrid mlkem768x25519 = `{ kem_ct: [bstr 1..64] (reassembling to 1120 bytes), wrap: bstr(48) }` (X-Wing encapsulation as a chunked byte array, with NO per-slot epk). Which field MUST/MUST NOT be present for the declared kem, each field's exact length, and rejection of cross-KEM contamination (an x25519 slot carrying kem_ct, or a hybrid slot carrying epk) are domain-pass checks emitting ENC_SLOT_INVALID_SHAPE / KEM_EPK_LENGTH_MISMATCH / KEM_CT_LENGTH_MISMATCH / WRAP_LENGTH_MISMATCH. CBOR byte strings are modelled as lowercase-hex JSON strings.

epk.$comment: Classical (x25519) ephemeral public key. 32-byte bstr. Present only on the classical path; absence/presence is enforced by the KEM-driven domain pass, not structurally.

kem_ct.$comment: Hybrid (mlkem768x25519) X-Wing encapsulation, carried chunked. Reassembled length (1120 bytes) is a domain-pass check.

wrap.$comment: Wrapped content-encryption key: 32-byte CEK + 16-byte AEAD tag = 48-byte bstr, on every KEM.
